markets.question: Exact Score: Nottingham Forest FC 0 - 3 Aston Villa FC?
markets.description: In the upcoming UEFA Europa League game between Nottingham Forest FC and Aston Villa FC, scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Nottingham Forest FC vs. Aston Villa FC match originally scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nottingham Forest host Aston Villa in the UEFA Europa League semi-final first leg at the City Ground on April 30, following their gritty 1-1 Premier League draw just five days ago where Neco Williams equalized for the hosts. Forest's recent advancement past Porto (2-1 aggregate, holding firm with 10 men despite a late red card) highlights defensive resilience at home, but injury knocks to Callum Hudson-Odoi (thigh/muscle), Murillo, and Chris Wood (knee concern) temper attacking threat. Villa demolished Bologna 4-0 (7-1 aggregate) with Ollie Watkins' milestone ton, yet Emi Martinez's recurring calf issue raises goalkeeper doubts. With Forest battling relegation (17th) and Villa pushing top-four (4th), trader consensus reflects a closely contested, low-scoring stalemate potential, bunching probabilities tightly amid balanced matchups and home edge.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both UB Alma Mater and ⁠Movistar KOI Fénix each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: UB Alma Mater vs ⁠Movistar KOI Fénix (BO3) - LES Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between UB Alma Mater and ⁠Movistar KOI Fénix in the LES Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 29 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "UB Alma Mater" if UB Alma Mater win the match against ⁠Movistar KOI Fénix.

This market will resolve to "⁠Movistar KOI Fénix" if ⁠Movistar KOI Fénix win the match against UB Alma Mater.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Movistar KOI Fénix enters this LES Spring Split BO3 matchup with a perfect 3-0 record and 6-1 games tally, second in standings behind Team Heretics Academy after sweeping LUA Gaming 2-0 on April 23, dominating Falke 2-0 on April 16, and edging Barça eSports 2-1 on April 8—showcasing strong macro play and consistency. UB Alma Mater languishes at 0-3 (0-6 games), winless following 0-2 losses to Falke on April 22, Barça on April 16, and GIANTX iTero on April 9, hampered by poor early-game execution. Movistar KOI Fénix previously swept UB 2-0 in February's Kick-off Tournament, reinforcing trader consensus on their edge in this single round-robin clash with playoffs looming for the top four.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both UB Alma Mater and ⁠Movistar KOI Fénix each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (HIGH) $158 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for EWY's May 2026 levels reflects robust momentum from South Korea's semiconductor sector, fueled by AI-driven demand for high-bandwidth memory chips, with Samsung Electronics reporting Q1 2026 revenue of KRW 133.9 trillion and surging profits announced April 29. The ETF trades near its 52-week high of $158.10, mirroring KOSPI's record-breaking rally past 6,700—up 44% year-to-date—bolstered by Middle East ceasefire hopes and Korea's ascent to the world's eighth-largest stock market by capitalization. Bank of Korea's steady 2.50% policy rate supports risk assets amid 1.8-2.0% GDP growth forecasts. Key May catalysts include the BoK's May 28 decision and Q2 earnings previews, with geopolitical flare-ups posing volatility risks.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Exort Series #26 Contenders Stage Swiss system, Privateer Gaming and Hermine Esports Club face off in a pivotal Round 2 lower-bracket Bo3, with the loser eliminated from the C-Tier online European CS2 event. Privateer dropped 0-2 to FORZE Reload in Round 1 (19-26 rounds, -7 RD), showing resilience on Ancient before falling on Overpass. Hermine likewise went 0-1 versus Oldboys on April 28, extending a skid from recent Exort outings including 0-2 losses to INFURITY and Phantom Academy. Hermine's fresh roster tweaks—adding fiction and kdaN three weeks ago—have yet to gel, per low map ratings on HLTV. A win sends either to 1-1, eyeing top-8 Main Stage advancement amid standard CS2 meta favoring execution on Inferno and Mirage.

markets.question: Coulibaly vs. Bennani: Match O/U 21.5
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 22. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."

eventMetadata.context_description: Fourth-seeded Eliakim Coulibaly, ranked No. 275 and playing on home soil in Abidjan, holds strong trader consensus as favorite over 18-year-old wildcard Karim Bennani (No. 575) in the ATP Challenger Abidjan 2 round of 16 on outdoor hard courts. Coulibaly advanced confidently with a 6-2, 7-6(4) win over Oskar Brostrom Poulsen in the previous round, leveraging his experience and surface familiarity. Bennani, in his Challenger main draw debut, impressed by upsetting Karan Singh but faces a steep ranking and seeding gap with no prior head-to-head. No injuries or withdrawals reported; local crowd support and Coulibaly's hard court form tilt probabilities heavily his way amid minimal recent shifts beyond tournament progression.

markets.question: Will Kimi Antonelli win the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix, scheduled for May 3, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 10, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli dominate trader consensus at 37% and 34.5% implied probabilities for the Miami Grand Prix driver winner, reflecting their 1-2 championship positions after three rounds—Antonelli leading with 72 points from poles and victories in China and Japan, Russell trailing by nine with consistent podiums. The razor-thin gap underscores intense intra-team rivalry on Mercedes' superior W17 package, with qualifying prowess and sprint shootout execution pivotal at the tight Miami International Autodrome. McLaren's Oscar Piastri (9.5%) and Lando Norris (7.5%) trail as credible threats via recent pace, while Ferrari's Leclerc and Hamilton lag amid setup struggles, keeping the outcome fiercely contested ahead of Friday's practice and sprint qualifying.

markets.question: Will SE Palmeiras win on 2026-05-10?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026
If SE Palmeiras wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Clube do Remo vs. SE Palmeiras
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Clube do Remo and SE Palmeiras.

eventMetadata.context_description: Palmeiras' strong second-place standing in Serie A, with 22 goals already amid solid away form (D W W L W), drives trader consensus to a 57% implied probability for victory over struggling 16th-placed Remo, who sit low on 13 goals despite recent home resilience including a 0-0 draw against Grêmio and a 4-1 upset win over Bahia. Remo's Baenão fortress boosts draw pricing to 26.5%, reflecting their L D W L D home streak and potential to frustrate via defensive setup. Ongoing Palmeiras injuries—Piquerez (ankle surgery), Vitor Roque (ankle), Jefté (thigh)—temper dominance without derailing favoritism, while limited head-to-head history leaves room for Remo's 21.5% upset potential in Belém's humid conditions.

markets.question: Will the DHS shutdown end between June 1-7 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the calendar date range (ET) of the end date of the Department of Homeland Security shutdown which began on February 14, 2026.

The end date of the shutdown will be determined by the date on which the funding bill required to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is signed by the President or otherwise enacted. The announcement of an impending reopen will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from official U.S. Government sources and a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: When will the DHS shutdown end?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the calendar date range (ET) of the end date of the Department of Homeland Security shutdown which began on February 14, 2026.

The end date of the shutdown will be determined by the date on which the funding bill required to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is signed by the President or otherwise enacted. The announcement of an impending reopen will not qualify.

The resolution sources for this market will be information from official U.S. Government sources and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: The House of Representatives overwhelmingly passed a Senate-approved funding bill on April 30, restoring appropriations to most of the Department of Homeland Security and ending its record 76-day partial shutdown that began February 14 amid immigration enforcement disputes, propelling trader consensus to 99.4% odds of resolution by May 3 as the measure heads to President Trump's desk for expected prompt signature. This bipartisan breakthrough followed weeks of Republican resistance and growing pressures from TSA backlogs, Coast Guard payment issues, and CISA cyber gaps, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in pricing near-certainty on swift enactment via standard appropriations process. Realistic challenges include a presidential veto, signing delay past May 3, or unforeseen procedural holds, though historical patterns favor quick closure once both chambers align.

markets.question: Will the Bahujan Samaj Party (BSP) win the most seats in the 2026 Puducherry Legislative Assembly election?
markets.description:
Legislative Assembly elections are to be scheduled to be held in Puducherry, India, in April–June 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly in the 2026 election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. If that also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (https://eci.gov.in). If multiple official reports differ, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the greatest number of Assembly Constituencies (ACs).

eventMetadata.context_description: Exit polls released April 29 project the incumbent All India N.R. Congress (AINRC)-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA), including BJP, securing 16-25 seats in Puducherry's 30-member Legislative Assembly, comfortably above the 16-seat majority mark needed to form government after the April 9 voting with record 89.9% turnout. This trader consensus at 96% for AINRC reflects the alliance's strong incumbency advantage under Chief Minister N. Rangasamy, fragmented opposition from the Congress-DMK INDIA bloc (projected 4-12 seats), and emerging TVK's limited impact. Results on May 4 could diverge if exit polls prove inaccurate due to historical margins of error, vote splits, or post-poll shifts, though such upsets remain low-probability.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy entered the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal BO3 against Infinite with strong trader backing, reflecting their perfect 2-0 group stage record (63-47 map score) versus Infinite's 2-1 finish (-9 map differential). Recent form favored Imperial's stability since their February roster lock-in, with key players like ta9z (1.12 rating) shining amid mixed results including a 2-1 win over MINLATE days prior. Infinite, ranked higher at #65 HLTV, showed momentum from topping United21 League Season 46 and recent roster additions like sl3nd (1.30 rating) and Dytor, but early exits against fnatic and Aurora tempered expectations. Online double-elimination format emphasized map pools, where Imperial held edges on Ancient and Nuke.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 12°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Ankara's May 2 highest temperature clusters tightly around 8°C (34%) and 9°C (31%), reflecting a rare arctic air intrusion funneled by a powerful omega block pattern over the North Atlantic, which has plunged central Anatolian temperatures 10–15°C below May norms of 20–22°C. The Turkish State Meteorological Service forecasts a daytime high near 11°C under mostly cloudy skies with 70–95% precipitation probability, suppressing solar heating and capping peaks; however, ensemble model spreads from ECMWF and GFS introduce uncertainty, with some runs suggesting thicker overcast or delayed clearing favoring 8°C, while drier scenarios edge toward 9–10°C. High humidity (60–85%) and 14 km/h winds further differentiate outcomes, with new MGM and global model updates expected daily ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Spain by April 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of the listed country between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

The truce has held without further direct Iranian attacks on Israel, despite strains from US naval blockades intercepting Iranian ships, reciprocal seizures through April 24, and Hezbollah drone strikes prompting Israeli responses in southern Lebanon as of April 26. Stalled US-Iran talks in Oman and Pakistan, IRGC hardline opposition, and April 30 Tehran rallies demanding an end to US threats underscore persistent escalation risks, with CENTCOM briefing potential renewed operations.

Forest's recent 5-0 rout of Sunderland and 4-1 win over Burnley signal momentum, alongside West Ham's 98th-minute triumph versus Everton, boosting trader consensus on their survival chances. markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Chair Powell emphasized these pressures as transitory, with core PCE at 3.2% tied to tariffs expected to fade, while labor markets show resilience at 4.3% unemployment amid low job gains. The March Summary of Economic Projections anticipates modest easing toward a low-3% range by year-end, aligning with futures implying steady rates around 3.6%. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI on May 12 and the June FOMC, where sustained inflation above 3% could challenge the no-hike base case.

Depor drew 1-1 at SD Huesca on April 12, extending an unbeaten run in their last five outings with solid defensive form, while hosting strong (eight wins in 17 home games). Leganés notched a 2-1 home win over Albacete on April 11 but lost 2-0 at UD Las Palmas on April 17, showing mixed away struggles. Their September 2-2 draw highlights a competitive head-to-head; Leganés nears recovery for defenders Rubén Peña and Jorge Sáenz from mid-April injuries, but Depor's momentum and home advantage shape trader consensus toward the hosts in this late-season clash.

markets.question: Will Mark Dixon win the 2026 Watford mayoral election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Watford mayoral election is currently scheduled to be held on May 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next mayor of Watford as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the Watford Borough Council.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Liberal Democrat Peter Taylor commands 94% trader consensus in the Watford Mayoral Election set for May 7 under first-past-the-post voting, reflecting his strong re-election prospects as the sitting mayor since 2018 with unanimous party re-selection last year and Liberal Democrat control of Watford Borough Council. No major developments, polls, or scandals have emerged in the past 30 days since candidate nominations, including a recent hustings event, leaving opposition fragmented among Labour's Keith Morgan, Conservative Abdul Laskar, Greens' Jake Mitchell, and minor parties or independents. While this year's vote precedes the council's 2028 unitary authority transition, low upset risk persists barring late turnout surges, voter mobilization against the incumbent, or unforeseen controversies.

markets.question: Will Luis Enrique be appointed as manager of Real Madrid?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of Real Madrid.

If no permanent manager is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other." 

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Real Madrid; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices José Mourinho as the clear frontrunner at 62% implied probability to become Real Madrid's next permanent manager, driven by reports that club president Florentino Pérez favors the Benfica coach—previously triumphant at the Bernabéu with La Décima—for a return amid Álvaro Arbeloa's faltering interim tenure since January's sacking of Xabi Alonso. Arbeloa, the ex-Castilla boss and former player, commands 49.5% as current head coach but faces mounting pressure after Real Madrid's Champions League elimination by Bayern Munich and slipping La Liga position, with Pérez reportedly set to act decisively post poor recent form. Lower odds for Julian Nagelsmann (15.3%) and Jürgen Klopp (10.5%) stem from weaker internal backing despite their pedigrees.

markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení hold a commanding position in Liga F with a near-perfect 24-0-1 record, propelled by recent thumpings of Real Madrid including a 3-0 quarter-final win on March 29 to secure their UEFA Women's Champions League semi-final spot against Bayern Frauen. Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from AtlasIntel (April 22–27) and Nexus (April 24–26) consistently show incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva leading the first round at 41–47%, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro in clear second at 36–40%, fueling trader consensus pricing Flávio at 66% for second place ahead of the October 4 vote. Lula's 21% implied probability for second reflects his incumbency advantage and polling lead, while Flávio's surge—narrowing the gap from earlier double-digits—stems from leveraging the Bolsonaro family brand amid a fragmented opposition field where candidates like Ronaldo Caiado (3–6%) and Romeu Zema (3–4%) trail far behind. Renan Santos garners minor support at 2–5%, aligning with low market odds. The tightening race underscores volatility from undecided voters (9–19%) and potential coalition shifts before the runoff trigger.

markets.question: Will Jonas Wind be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season?
markets.description:

Goals scored in Bundesliga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., DFB-Pokal, German Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy enters the Exort Series #25 Main Stage BO3 matchup against Wave Esports with a refreshed roster since February, anchored by veteran rifler Kiryasoo (0.98 HLTV rating over 45 maps) and coached by S0tF1k (59% career win rate), leveraging Team Spirit's academy infrastructure for structured CS2 play. Wave Esports, ranked #149 globally, fields a March-overhauled lineup featuring newcomers Belof (1.05 rating), Katalic, and sheezy alongside miksozzz, amid a 37.5% win rate in recent tier-3 events like Urban Riga Open and Esplay Backforce Masters. In Swiss Round 1, factors include adaptation to CS2 meta on maps like Dust II, Mirage, and Ancient, pistol round execution, and roster synergy, with no recent head-to-head history shaping trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) hit (LOW) $280 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.GOOGL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: The pitching matchup anchors trader focus for Thursday's makeup game at Citizens Bank Park, with Philadelphia's Cristopher Sánchez (2-2, 2.94 ERA) facing San Francisco's Logan Webb (2-3, 4.86 ERA) in a battle favoring the lefty's recent command. Phillies gained momentum shutting out the Giants 7-0 on April 28 behind strong starters and bullpen work, though Tuesday's contest fell to inclement weather; both clubs sit below .500 (Giants 13-16, Phillies 10-19) amid NL races. Philadelphia catcher J.T. Realmuto remains sidelined by back spasms (10-day IL), forcing Rafael Marchán behind the plate, while Giants' outfield depth is tested by Harrison Bader's hamstring strain. Home-field edge and Sánchez's form tilt sentiment toward the hosts in this low-standings clash.

markets.question: Will Charles Leclerc win the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the winner of the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix, scheduled for May 3, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 10, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially listed in first place in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The Final Classification is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the Final Classification will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

The razor-thin gap underscores intense intra-team rivalry on Mercedes' superior W17 package, with qualifying prowess and sprint shootout execution pivotal at the tight Miami International Autodrome. McLaren's Oscar Piastri (9.5%) and Lando Norris (7.5%) trail as credible threats via recent pace, while Ferrari's Leclerc and Hamilton lag amid setup struggles, keeping the outcome fiercely contested ahead of Friday's practice and sprint qualifying.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $1.60 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=ngd, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices stand at $2.72 per MMBtu as of April 27, 2026, with nearby May futures at $2.775 amid a 5% weekly rally driven by cooler shoulder-season weather forecasts boosting heating demand. Abundant inventories reached 2,063 Bcf after a 103 Bcf injection for the week ended April 17—exceeding consensus estimates and underscoring a storage surplus versus five-year averages. Steady dry gas production near 110 Bcf/d and robust LNG exports to Europe maintain downward pressure, while the futures curve in mild contango reflects expectations of summer builds. Traders eye today's EIA storage report (week ended April 24, est. +79-83 Bcf) and end-April settlement for final pricing catalysts.

markets.question: Will Moderna (MRNA) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Moderna is estimated to release earnings on May 1, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Moderna's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-2.67 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Moderna reports GAAP EPS greater than $-2.67 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Moderna releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

Positive developments, such as the April 21 Phase 3 initiation for an mRNA flu vaccine, provide longer-term optimism but little near-term lift. Earnings release looms on May 1, with focus on revenue trajectory and cost controls.

markets.question: Will xAI have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Serie A for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Serie B, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by Serie A and Serie B.

If the 2025-2026 Serie B season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

Monza and Frosinone, tied on 75 points, vie intensely for the second direct spot, bolstered by Frosinone's better +36 GD, while Palermo sits fourth on 69 points eyeing strong playoff seeding. Recent matchday 36 results solidified these positions, with no major injuries reported among contenders. The final two rounds before May 8 could shift the automatic duo and playoff bracket—preliminary ties on May 12 pit fifth-placed Catanzaro against eighth-placed Avellino, and sixth versus seventh—before semis and a promotion final, emphasizing home form and head-to-head edges in the wisdom-of-crowds pricing.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Denver be between 44-45°F on May 1?

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's coalition government passed the 2026 state budget on April 26, averting automatic Knesset dissolution and snap elections that could have triggered his ouster. This legislative win has temporarily stabilized his narrow majority amid persistent internal tensions, including haredi draft exemptions and opposition surges in recent polls, where a new Bennett-Lapid alliance has gained traction. With legislative elections due by late October 2026, traders monitor risks from no-confidence votes, coalition defections, and public backlash over security operations against Hezbollah—highlighted by recent tunnel destructions and warnings of sabotage to Lebanon peace talks—plus lingering effects from the Iran ceasefire. Netanyahu's early-stage prostate cancer treatment concluded successfully this month without impacting duties.

markets.question: Susie Wiles out as White House Chief of Staff by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Susie Wiles ceases to be the White House Chief of Staff for any period of time between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Wiles' resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Claudia López win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices unidentified moderate Candidate M as narrow favorite at 49.5% to win Colombia's May 31 presidential first round, amid fragmented opposition and skepticism toward recent polls showing Iván Cepeda Castro leading at 38-44% in surveys like Invamer and AtlasIntel from late April. Cepeda benefits from President Petro's resurgence in approval ratings and the Historic Pact's congressional gains in March primaries and legislative elections, positioning him strongly for a potential June 21 runoff. Right-wing challengers Paloma Valencia (33.7%, Centro Democrático primary winner) and Abelardo de la Espriella (28.5%, populist outsider) split conservative votes, keeping the race closely contested with no candidate polling a first-round majority.

eventMetadata.context_description: Min Hyung-bae commands 99.4% trader consensus to win the inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Special City mayoral election on June 3, driven by the Democratic Party of Korea's overwhelming historical dominance in Gwangju and South Jeolla province, a region where People Power Party challengers like Lee Jung-hyun rarely exceed low-single-digit support. The Democratic Party confirmed Min as nominee on April 14 after primaries that unified contenders, while the People Power Party selected Lee on April 22 amid limited local enthusiasm. A major scandal, health issue, or late endorsement surge could theoretically challenge this, though structural regional dynamics favor the incumbent party heavily.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 has rallied to fresh highs near 7,180, reflecting trader consensus on economic resilience after March nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs—exceeding forecasts—with unemployment holding at 4.3%. However, hotter-than-expected March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year has tempered rate cut bets ahead of the just-concluded April 28-29 FOMC meeting. Strong corporate earnings, including Intel's beat, bolster AI-driven sector gains, while analyst estimates project 14-17% earnings-per-share growth through year-end. Key catalysts include June 16-17 FOMC, quarterly CPI and payrolls releases, and Q2 earnings season, with geopolitical tensions posing downside risks to valuation multiples.

markets.question: Will Manchester City FC win on 2026-05-09?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 9, 2026
If Manchester City FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Brentford FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 9, 2026 between Manchester City FC and Brentford FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City enter their Etihad Stadium clash with Brentford as clear trader favorites at 74.5% implied probability, driven by their position atop the tight Premier League title race alongside Arsenal after a pivotal 2-1 win over the Gunners on April 19 and subsequent victories against Burnley and Southampton. Despite ongoing defensive absences—Ruben Dias sidelined with an ankle issue, Josko Gvardiol recovering, and John Stones doubtful—youngsters Marc Guehi and Abdukodir Khusanov have solidified the backline, bolstering squad depth. Brentford languish mid-table in ninth, hampered by a run of draws including 0-0 versus Fulham on April 18 and multiple injuries like Aaron Hickey's hamstring and Vitaly Janelt's metatarsal problems, alongside City's dominant 7-2-1 head-to-head record. The Bees' upset potential remains slim given poor away form against top sides, pricing the draw at 14.5% and victory at 10.5%.

markets.question: Will Republicanos (REPUBLICANOS) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices PL at 76% to secure the most seats among the 54 contested in Brazil's October 4 Senate election, reflecting the party's frontrunners in recent state polls like Santa Catarina (Carol de Toni leading at 23%) and Rio de Janeiro (Carlos Bolsonaro at 25%), alongside projections of 20-22 wins from state-by-state analyses. Opposition momentum surged from last week's AtlasIntel (April 22-27) and BTG/Nexus (April 24-26) presidential polls showing Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) statistically tied with Lula in runoffs, signaling strong right-wing mobilization. UNIÃO and REPUBLICANOS linger as challengers in southern races, while PT trails amid weaker Northeast showings; primaries and campaign shifts could alter dynamics ahead of the renewal impacting the next government's Senate balance.

markets.question: Will President Trump sign 3 pieces of legislation into law in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of pieces of legislation that are signed into law between April 1, 2026, 12:00 AM and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Qualifying legislation may include joint resolutions and must pass both the House and the Senate, and must be signed by the President. 

Legislation that becomes law without a signature while Congress remains in session, or becomes law through veto override, as well as Presidential pocket vetoes that expire, will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/), Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: How many pieces of legislation will Trump sign into law in April?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of pieces of legislation that are signed into law between April 1, 2026, 12:00 AM and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Qualifying legislation may include joint resolutions and must pass both the House and the Senate, and must be signed by the President. 

Legislation that becomes law without a signature while Congress remains in session, or becomes law through veto override, as well as Presidential pocket vetoes that expire, will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/), Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors six pieces of legislation signed into law by President Trump in April at 56%, reflecting five already enacted—S. 1884 and S. 3971 on April 13, H.R. 8322 extending FISA surveillance authorities on April 18, and H.J.Res. 140 disapproving a Bureau of Land Management rule on April 27—amid expectations of one more before month's end. Recent House passage of a Senate-backed DHS funding bill to avert shutdown heightens prospects for swift signature, consistent with rapid clearance of noncontroversial measures like Congressional Review Act resolutions and minor authorizations. Odds on five at 13% acknowledge potential delays, while lower probabilities for fewer underscore the administration's pattern of high-volume bill signings on routine priorities, bolstered by Republican congressional majorities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on major tech IPOs before 2027 hinges on surging expectations for a blockbuster 2026 listing wave, propelled by AI and space sector valuations exceeding $1 trillion for frontrunners like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic. Recent reports highlight SpaceX eyeing a June 2026 debut at up to $1.75 trillion, while Databricks targets year-end amid data intelligence demand, and fintechs Stripe and Revolut prepare amid rebounding public markets—following Klarna's 2025 NYSE listing. Competitive pressures among AI labs and improving economic conditions drive optimism, though historical delays from regulatory scrutiny or volatility persist. Watch for S-1 filings and Q2 earnings for timeline signals, with resolution hinging on priced offerings by December 31, 2026.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the CA-31 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-31 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Gil Cisneros holds a commanding position in California's 31st Congressional District, a D+8 seat per Cook Partisan Voter Index, driving trader consensus to 92.5% for the Democratic Party ahead of the June 2 top-two primary. His 59.7% victory in 2024, combined with a fundraising lead—$111,660 cash on hand as of late 2025 versus Republican challengers Eric Ching's $69,445 and Erskine Levi's $0—bolsters his path to the general election on November 3. Cook Political Report rates it Solid Democratic, citing his shift from a prior competitive district. While a GOP primary upset or national Republican wave could challenge this, significant barriers like the partisan lean and incumbent advantage make shifts unlikely barring scandals, legal issues, or health events.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 57% probability for People Power Party lawmaker Choo Kyung-ho to win the Daegu mayoral election on June 3, ahead of Democratic Party former Prime Minister Kim Boo-kyum at 43%, buoyed by the city's status as a conservative stronghold where PPP has historically dominated despite Kim's outsider appeal. Recent polls reflect a tight contest: Daegu MBC/Korea Research (April 28-29) shows Kim leading 44%-35% outside the margin of error, while Maeil Shinmun/Hangil (April 27-28) has Choo edging 46%-43% within it, highlighting generational divides with Kim strong among 40s-50s and Choo among seniors amid voter focus on economic activation and regional development pledges.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 275M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US commercial crude oil inventories dropped 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24 per the latest EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report, propelled by record exports, refinery inputs averaging 16.1 million barrels per day at 89.6% utilization, and lower imports of 5.8 million barrels per day—still 1% above the five-year average. This draw follows prior builds amid volatile global supply from OPEC+ production cuts and Middle East disruptions tightening worldwide stocks. Heading into summer driving season with Memorial Day on May 25, seasonal gasoline demand could accelerate declines. Traders track weekly EIA reports (next May 6) and Tuesday API previews through the June 4 release covering data by June 5 for resolution.

markets.question: Will Demeke Mekonnen be the next Prime Minister of Ethiopia?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Ethiopia on June 1, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Ethiopia following the 2026 General elections.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Ethiopia. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2028, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Ethiopia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed commands 91% trader consensus ahead of Ethiopia's general election on June 1, driven by his Prosperity Party's entrenched control of institutions, re-election as party leader in early 2025, and a fragmented opposition unable to mount a credible challenge. Recent National Election Board preparations, including extended voter registration deadlines and Prosperity Party's strategic choice to skip Tigray constituencies entirely while leaving select Amhara opposition areas uncontested as of late March, underscore logistical momentum without disrupting Abiy's path to another parliamentary majority. Ongoing regional tensions in Amhara and Oromia persist but have not eroded his position; scenarios like widespread violence, election irregularities, or a surprise opposition coalition could narrow the gap, though historical landslide precedents favor continuity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at a near-certain 97.7% for a Bill Clinton divorce by June 30, driven by the total absence of legal filings, public announcements, or verified separation reports from the Clintons despite late-2025 tabloid rumors tied to Epstein documents. markets.question: Will United Kingdom come in last place at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 places last in the final.

If no last place country is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.
